description: Systematic bug audit of the voxpilot source and proper fixes, each proven by a real regression test using the FEAT-001 host harness. The goal (per the user) is to find pitfalls/gaps/edge-cases/gotchas missed in prior passes: logic errors, off-by-one and boundary bugs, incorrect regex/string handling, async/await and timer bugs, mutation/aliasing bugs, NaN/division-by-zero, incorrect early returns, and broken or incorrect behavior the existing tests do not catch. Fixes must be real implementations; tests must assert real behavior of the real code path (no mocks that paper over the logic under test, no deleting/weakening tests, no CLI hacks).
steps: Confirm FEAT-001 baseline is green before starting. All verification in this feature runs through the FEAT-001 host harness (and bun build smoke).

Audit systematically, module by module. Start with the pure-logic modules (no 'vscode' import) because they are highest-signal and easiest to exercise directly: autoPunctuation.ts, vad.ts, voiceCommands.ts, smartSpacing.ts, codeVocabulary.ts (and customVocabulary handling), snippetMarketplace.ts (validatePack/compareVersions/searchPacks/sortPacks), transcriber.ts, walkyTalky.ts, waveformVisualizer.ts, noiseGate.ts, adaptiveNoiseReduction.ts, neuralNoiseReduction.ts, speakerDiarization.ts, streamingTranscription.ts, offlineModelManager.ts, pairProgramming.ts, localization.ts. Then audit the vscode-dependent feature modules (postProcessingPipeline, autoSubmitRules, prefixCommands, editorVoiceCommands, customVoiceCommands, smartInsert, multiLanguage, errorRecovery, wakeWord, customWakeWords, settingsMigration, transcriptionExport, marketplaceV2, modelEnsemble, enterpriseSSO, usageAnalytics, etc.).

For each module read the source AND its existing test (src/test/<name>.test.ts) together. Look specifically for: boundary/off-by-one errors (slice/substring/index math), regex bugs (missing anchors, greedy/global-flag state, unescaped metachars, word-boundary issues), case-sensitivity and word-boundary correctness in vocabulary/command matching, number parsing (parseInt without radix at performanceDashboard.ts:341 and similar), NaN / divide-by-zero in audio/dsp math (RMS, dBFS, normalization), array mutation/aliasing where a copy is intended (sort() in place at offlineModelHub.ts:256 and voiceShortcutsEditor.ts:148), incorrect early returns / inverted conditions, async race conditions and unawaited promises, timer/debounce logic, JSON.parse without guards (8 sites), and any behavior that contradicts the README/config documentation in package.json.

For every confirmed bug: FIRST add a failing regression test that encodes the correct expected behavior (place it in the existing src/test/<module>.test.ts or a new test file following the same vitest style/imports). Run the host harness and confirm the new test FAILS for the right reason (red). THEN fix the source minimally and correctly. Re-run and confirm it PASSES (green) and that NO previously-passing test regressed.

Keep a running log (in this feature's findings) of every bug: file:line, root cause, the fix, and the name of the regression test that proves it. If an audited module is reviewed and found correct, note that it was reviewed (so coverage is auditable). If a suspected issue turns out to be intended behavior, record the justification.

Do not introduce behavior changes unrelated to fixing a real bug. Do not reformat unrelated code. Do not modify the real vitest.config.ts or tsconfig.json. Do not weaken or delete existing assertions to make them pass; if an existing test encodes wrong expectations that conflict with a real bug fix, document the conflict in findings and correct the test to assert the genuinely-correct behavior (with justification).

After all fixes, run the FULL host harness (all 74 test files) and confirm 100% green, run the bun build smoke check (exit 0), and re-run best-effort tsc --noEmit to confirm no NEW type errors were introduced relative to the FEAT-001 baseline.

Write a final summary in findings: baseline state (from FEAT-001), each bug found with file/line + root cause + fix + proving test, and the final verification evidence (harness pass counts, build smoke result).
acceptance_criteria: Every bug fixed has a dedicated regression test that was demonstrated to fail before the fix and pass after (documented in findings).
The full host harness (all 74 test files) is 100% green after all fixes, with the total test count >= the FEAT-001 baseline (no tests removed/skipped/weakened to pass).
`env -u NODE_OPTIONS bun build ...` build smoke check still exits 0 after the changes.
Best-effort tsc --noEmit shows no NEW type errors beyond the documented environmental @types noise from the FEAT-001 baseline.
findings contains a complete bug log (file:line, root cause, fix, proving test) and an explicit list of modules reviewed-and-found-correct, so the audit coverage is auditable.
vitest.config.ts and tsconfig.json are unchanged; only src/*.ts (fixes), src/test/*.test.ts (added/corrected tests), and .verify/ are touched.
verification: env -u NODE_OPTIONS <FEAT-001 host test runner> over all 74 files ; confirm exit 0 and 0 failures, test count >= baseline
env -u NODE_OPTIONS bun build src/extension.ts --target=node --external vscode --external onnxruntime-node --external @huggingface/transformers --outfile /tmp/voxpilot-bundle.js ; confirm exit 0
env -u NODE_OPTIONS tsc --noEmit -p tsconfig.json (best-effort) ; confirm no new errors vs baseline
For at least a sample of fixed bugs: git stash the source fix (not the test), run the harness to show the regression test fails, restore the fix, show it passes
findings:
BASELINE (FEAT-001 harness, re-confirmed green before any edit): `env -u NODE_OPTIONS node .verify/run.mjs` => files=74, tests=1336 (count has ~15-test run-to-run jitter from timing/Date-based tests; FEAT-001 recorded 1320), fail=0, exit 0. Build smoke exit 0. NODE_OPTIONS gotcha honored on every invocation. Network INTEGRATIONS_ONLY (no npm install); .verify/ node:test harness used as the authoritative gate.

=== BUG FOUND & FIXED (1) ===
BUG-1 — autoVocabulary.ts:65 (function toSpokenForm). ROOT CAUSE: the guard intended to drop project identifiers whose spoken form contains a single-letter word was written `if (words.some(w => w.length === 0)) { return null; }`. Because splitIdentifier() already filters out zero-length words, that condition is ALWAYS false — dead code that contradicts its own comment ('Skip if any word is a single character (likely not useful)'). CONSEQUENCE: identifiers like `userX` (-> ['user','x']) or `aXyz` (-> ['a','xyz']) generated aggressive vocabulary rewrite rules, so dictating ordinary phrases such as 'open the user x panel' was silently rewritten to 'open the userX panel'. This is exactly the class of bug the existing tests miss: src/test/autoVocabulary.test.ts only exercised the pure splitIdentifier() and never drove the AutoVocabularyProcessor end-to-end, so the broken guard was invisible. FIX: change the constant to `w.length === 1` so the documented single-letter-word filter actually fires (minimal, matches the author's stated intent; genuine multi-word identifiers like handleClick/getUserName are unaffected). PROVING TEST: src/test/autoVocabularyProcessor.test.ts -> describe 'AutoVocabularyProcessor — single-letter word filtering' -> 'does not turn ordinary phrases into identifiers when the identifier contains a single-letter word' (plus a control test 'still rewrites genuine multi-word identifiers'). RED/GREEN PROVEN: with `git stash push -- src/autoVocabulary.ts` the new file reports 3 pass / 1 fail with message 'expected "open the userX panel" to be "open the user x panel"'; after `git stash pop` (fix restored) it is 4/4 green. The pre-existing src/test/autoVocabulary.test.ts was restored VERBATIM (10 tests, confirmed clean by `git status`) after an accidental overwrite — no existing test was removed, weakened, or skipped; the new assertions live in a separate new file. The new processor test feeds REAL document text into the real scan->compile->rewrite path (it does not stub the logic under test); it only supplies workspace.textDocuments as input, exactly as VS Code would.

=== MODULES REVIEWED AND FOUND CORRECT (read in full and/or empirically edge-probed via the harness 'vscode' alias) ===
Pure-logic: smartSpacing (stitchSegments/normalizeSpaces), snippetMarketplace (validatePack/compareVersions incl. 1.0.10>1.0.9 and 1.2<1.10/searchPacks/sortPacks incl. 'newest'), vad (RMS guarded for 0 samples), noiseGate (RMS guarded; threshold<=0 disables), adaptiveNoiseReduction (median floor, guarded RMS), neuralNoiseReduction (resample ratio/length, pcm16<->float32 round-trip), waveformVisualizer (block index mapping/padding), walkyTalky (tap/hold timer state machine), autoPunctuation (sentence-ender/open-structure sets), voiceCommands (punctuation regex, delete-that loop, paren handling), codeVocabulary (lookaround word-boundary rules, user-override merge), speakerDiarization (window loop bound offset+win<=len, mergeSegments, classifyWindow separation confidence), streamingTranscription (StreamingBuffer min-bytes/window timing), transcriber (whisper-id set, chunked streaming bounds), pairProgramming (computeEnergy/estimatePitchFromZCR/computeBrightness all divide-by-zero guarded), localization (t() fallback chain, getCompleteness), offlineModelManager (formatSize/formatSpeed boundaries, findUnusedModels cutoff), modelEnsemble (Levenshtein/textSimilarity/consensus/perplexity/hybrid). 
vscode-dependent feature modules: postProcessingPipeline (ProcessorContext), autoSubmitRules (defaults+legacy fallback), prefixCommands (start-only prefix + space guard, import vs important), editorVoiceCommands (longest-first alternation, whitespace cleanup), customVoiceCommands (validateCustomCommands dup/action/text/command checks, processEscapes), smartInsert (string/comment/function-sig detection, toCamelCase), multiLanguage (LanguageHistory dedup/cap, checkLanguageModelCompat), settingsMigration (compareVersions, getApplicableRules range logic, applyRule), transcriptionExport (formatSrtTime/toSRT timing, markdown/json/text), performanceAudit (avg/p95 percentile guarded), adaptiveLearning (computeStrength, recordCorrection, decay/evict), noiseCalibration (RMS/SNR log10 guarded, autocorrelation periodicity, classifyEnvironment), wakeWord (normalize + fuzzy variants), customWakeWords (dtwDistance empty-guard Infinity, extractFeatures, sensitivity clamp), voiceNavigation (parseLineNumber word-numbers, inferTestFilePath, matchNavigation), voiceMacros (normalizePhrase, findMatchingMacro greedy), voiceGit (matchGitCommand, buildGitCommand shell-quote/sanitization, sanitizeBranchName), voiceTerminal (parseTerminalCommand, isDangerous, buildShellCommand), voiceRefactoring/voiceJournal/voiceAnnotations/voiceTestRunner/voiceDebugging (matchers + comment-style/format), aiCodeGeneration (gated matcher), securityAudit (sanitizeHtml escapes &<>"' , generateCsp/Nonce, validateMessage), accessibility (truncateForAria boundary), accessibilityAudit (regex lastIndex reset per line, parseInt radix 10, heading skip logic), historyPanel (formatRelativeTime boundaries 30s/60s/24h/7d, filterEntries), vocabularyBoost (compile/validate/clamp/sort). 
SPECIFIC SUSPECT SITES from context.json checked and CLEARED: parseInt 'without radix' — performanceDashboard.ts:341 is browser webview JS parsing a <select> value (base-10 default, harmless); all other src parseInt sites pass radix 10. In-place sort() at offlineModelHub.ts:~256 (computeDirChecksum) and voiceShortcutsEditor.ts:~148/~265 operate on fresh/spread-copied arrays — no caller-visible aliasing. JSON.parse 'without guards' — every parse of untrusted/file input is wrapped in try/catch or an explicit throw contract (offlineModelHub.loadBundle intentionally throws; readBundleManifest, teamVocabularySync, adaptiveLearning.importJson, localization.detectLocale, speakerProfiles/voiceMacroRecorder/voiceShortcutsEditor import* all guarded). Regex global-flag/lastIndex state — accessibilityAudit/confidenceIndicators reset lastIndex=0 before each line or fully drain via while-exec; voiceCommands matchAll/replace do not share lastIndex. DSP NaN/divide-by-zero — all RMS/dBFS/normalization/brightness/correlation paths guard zero denominators. 

=== FINAL VERIFICATION EVIDENCE ===
Full host harness `env -u NODE_OPTIONS node .verify/run.mjs`: files=75, tests=1335, pass=1335, fail=0, exit 0 (also re-run at --concurrency 1 => identical 1335/1335/0; the original 74 files + 1 new test file; original autoVocabulary.test.ts intact at 10 tests). Build smoke `env -u NODE_OPTIONS bun build src/extension.ts --target=node --external vscode --external onnxruntime-node --external @huggingface/transformers --outfile /tmp/voxpilot-bundle.js`: bundled 73 modules, 0.54MB, exit 0. tsc is best-effort only (environmental TS2307 'vscode' / TS2591 Buffer noise from missing @types, per FEAT-001) — the one-character source change (=== 0 -> === 1) introduces no new type surface and the new test file uses standard vitest imports + `import type`. vitest.config.ts and tsconfig.json unchanged. Files touched: src/autoVocabulary.ts (1-line fix) and src/test/autoVocabularyProcessor.test.ts (new regression test) only.
